title: Connection resilience: stale daemon, suspended SW, port collision, pending hangs
description: Recurring symptom: Claude reports 'MCP isn't seeing a connection'. Root causes: (A) MV3 service worker suspended → ws.readyState lies as OPEN while Chrome silently killed the socket; passive keepalive doesn't detect this. (B) Stale daemon: ensureDaemon only checks port-open; after rebuild, old daemon stays loaded in memory. (C) No-browser error has no diagnostic guidance. (D) When daemon dies mid-call, pending relay requests hang until timeout instead of failing fast. Fixes: /version endpoint with binary modtime so MCP instance detects stale daemon and respawns; active WS ping/pong in extension; better no-browser error; bail pending on relay disconnect.
close_reason: Shipped in PR #6 — daemon /version + binary-mtime stale detection, active extension ping/pong, fail-fast pending on relay disconnect, port-collision diagnostics, no-browser error rewrite

title: Authenticate relay clients beyond localhost
description:
Currently any process that can open a localhost TCP socket can connect to ws://127.0.0.1:18321/relay, send a `{type:"register", label:"..."}` message, and have its commands attributed to a fake agent label in the extension popup. A malicious local extension or local script can also drive the browser while masquerading as Claude Code.

Surface: ws.go:142-159 (handleRelayConnection) accepts any register payload verbatim. The CheckOrigin policy already restricts WS origins to localhost / chrome-extension://, but that's a coarse boundary.

Remediation options (pick one):
- Derive the label from the relay client's resolved PID via SO_PEERCRED (Linux) / libproc (macOS). Reject mismatches.
- Issue a token from the daemon on first start (written to ~/.config/turboweb-mcp-by-ikari/relay.token, mode 0600). Require it on register.
- Extension-id allowlist for chrome-extension:// origins.

Why this matters: the popup is the user's only attribution UI. If we can't trust the label, the multi-Claude feature becomes a misleading affordance.

Found by: security-sentinel review of PR #3.

title: cdp_type/cdp_scroll: accept selector (focus / wheel-at-centre)
description: Mirror the cdp_click selector treatment for cdp_type (focus selector before typing, verify activeElement moved) and cdp_scroll (resolve selector to centre, dispatch wheel there so the nearest scrollable ancestor receives it — for inner dropdowns / virtualised lists). Extracted cdpResolveSelectorCenter (JS) and focusSelector (Go) helpers. Selector wins when both selector and coords are provided.

title: cdp_click: accept selector OR x,y (selector wins, resolves to centre)
description: Extended cdp_click to take a CSS selector that's resolved on the page side via getBoundingClientRect and clicked at its centre — previously coords-only. Useful pair with the javascript: hint from synthetic click. Added bidiEvaluateJSON + resolveSelectorCenter helpers (Go) and selector path in cdpClick (extension). Tested both null-match and zero-bbox failure modes.

title: intercept_file_chooser MCP tool (Page.setInterceptFileChooserDialog)
description: Arm/disarm interception of native file chooser dialogs and auto-fulfil with a preset path list. Useful when the file input is gnarly to select (cross-frame, lazy-mounted, dispatched-via-button). API: intercept_file_chooser({ enable: bool, files?: string[] }) — enabling registers a Page.setInterceptFileChooserDialog handler that calls DOM.setFileInputFiles with backendNodeId from the Page.fileChooserOpened event. Per-tab state in the extension; survives until disarmed or debugger detaches.

title: set_input_files MCP tool (CDP DOM.setFileInputFiles)
description: Attach files to <input type=file> via CDP DOM.setFileInputFiles. Works on hidden/styled inputs, triggers change event as if the user picked. Path resolution policy: tilde-expansion, reject relative paths, realpath through symlinks, verify file exists — all in the Go server before forwarding to the extension. Validate multiple attribute (>1 path needs <input multiple>). Resolved selector should auto-walk to nearest descendant input[type=file] if the selector hit a wrapper/label.

title: clickElement: skip default nav on a[href^=javascript:] to dodge page CSP
description: On pages with strict CSP (no unsafe-inline, no unsafe-hashes), clicking <a href="javascript:void(0)" onclick="..."> fails with 'Running the JavaScript URL violates the following Content Security Policy directive'. The synthetic click triggers the browser's default activation, which navigates to the javascript: URL, which CSP blocks. Fix: detect href^=javascript: on the target anchor and preventDefault the click via a one-shot capture listener so the page's onclick handler still fires but the navigation is suppressed. Repro: https://connectmls8.mredllc.com/photographer/managePhotosNew.jsp
close_reason: Returns hint to use cdp_click when target is <a href=javascript:...> — fix shipped in extension/content.js

title: Element-anchor agent cursor overlay on scroll
description: Currently the agent cursor overlay in extension/content.js is viewport-fixed (position: fixed) like a real mouse. But the agent cursor represents what the agent is acting on, not where the user is looking — so when the user scrolls after an action, the cursor floats over unrelated content instead of staying glued to the targeted element. Fix: after each action, store the target element ref and update the cursor's top/left from getBoundingClientRect() on scroll + resize until the next action (or the 45s idle fade).

title: Make custom-tool DB migration atomic (temp + rename)
description:
tools_custom.go:78-83 currently does:
  data, _ := os.ReadFile(legacyDB)
  os.WriteFile(dbPath, data, 0644)

Two issues:
1. Atomicity — a crash mid-write leaves a half-copied sqlite file at the target. Next launch sees the target exists, the legacy-migration branch doesn't fire again, and the user is stuck with a corrupted DB.
2. Permissions — mode 0644 is world-readable. Custom-tool DBs can contain JS the user pasted in. Should be 0600.

Fix: write to dbPath + ".tmp" with mode 0600, fsync, os.Rename to dbPath atomically. ~10 lines.

Found by: performance-oracle + security-sentinel review of PR #3.

title: connection_status reports 0 browsers in relay mode
description: handleConnectionStatus reads the MCP-instance-local getOpenBrowsers() map, but in relay mode that map is always empty — the real browsers map lives in the daemon process. Tool always returns {connected: false, extension: false, browsers: []} when relay is in use, even when list_tabs and every other tool work perfectly. Fix: route connection_status through send() like the other tools, with a daemon-side handler that returns the daemon's view.

title: Toast stack: max 2 visible, min 15s lifetime, stacked slide-up animation
description: Replace singleton toast with a 2-deep stack. Each toast lives at least 15s after appearance; if the action runs longer, the toast stays until showResult/showError fires. New action slides the previous toast up to a 'stacked' slot above the bottom slot; any 3rd toast evicts the oldest. Safety: 60s hard max-life to avoid orphans if end events are dropped. Why: toasts are click-through (pointer-events: none) so users can't pause-on-hover; bumping the lifetime fixes that without making them blockers.

title: Toast text swaps instantly on rapid showToast — add fade-out/swap/fade-in
description:
content.js:showToast clears the previous toastTimer, rewrites innerHTML, re-arms .on, sets fresh 7s timer. If a new showToast lands while .on is true (likely now that lifetime is 7s, longer than many agent action chains), the user sees the toast text swap instantaneously without a transition beat. CSS opacity transition is 120ms but text changes are synchronous mid-lifetime.

Fix: brief fade-out → swap → fade-in, OR per-message id that ignores re-shows while the current toast is still readable, OR a small queue.

Found by: julik-frontend-races review of PR #5.

title: Per-row UI state loss on every popup rerenderLog
description:
popup.js replaces the entire log children on every command start AND every command done (2× per command). Even with the new replaceChildren one-reflow optimization (PR #4), per-row state is destroyed every time: expanded/collapsed detail panels reset, focus on a row is lost, text selection within a row disappears, scroll position WITHIN a row is reset.

Fix: keyed diff — keep entry DOM nodes keyed by id, only update changed ones. Adds ~50 LOC but removes the rebuild churn entirely.

Found by: performance-oracle review of PR #4.

title: sel() result is not stable across DOM mutations between calls
description:
Two consecutive sel() invocations on the same element can return different selectors if a sibling was inserted/removed (changes :nth-of-type indexing) or if an ancestor gained/lost an id between calls. Callers in background.js round-trip selectors back to the page — the cursor animation in resolveTarget re-resolves via the returned selector, and the real clickElement re-resolves again. On an SPA that mutates aggressively, the cursor can land on one element while the click lands on another.

Two paths forward:
1. Document explicitly that sel() outputs are point-in-time advisory and the agent should treat them as ephemeral.
2. Capture a WeakRef to the original element alongside the selector and prefer the WeakRef when still attached.

Found by: julik-frontend-races review of PR #5.

title: Memoise sel() per tool call via WeakMap
description:
content.js:sel() runs up to 15 document.querySelectorAll() probes per call. queryElements / getInteractiveMap call sel() per result element. Worst case on a page with 500 interactive elements: 500 × 15 = 7500 full-document selector evaluations per get_interactive_map call.

Mitigation: cache sel() result on a WeakMap keyed by element for the duration of a single tool call. Pass the WeakMap into the helper and clear it between tool invocations, or scope it to the function-call site.

Not a race; contributes to perceptible UI hitches that could interact with the 900ms cursor-arrival gate.

Found by: julik-frontend-races review of PR #5.

title: scanLoupe + overlapping showStart: single-flight the cursor animation
description:
content.js:scanLoupe is awaitable but unguarded against re-entry. A new showStart that calls moveCursorTo while a scanLoupe sweep is mid-iteration will share the global cursorPos and the cursor.style.transform — two rAF step loops fight, with the newer one resetting 'start' from a cursorPos the older loop hasn't committed yet. Visually: the cursor jerks.

Capped at 6 items so worst case ~4.5s of contended animation — enough time for a fast agent to fire a follow-up. Worth a single-flight guard or an AbortController on the active animation.

Found by: julik-frontend-races review of PR #5.

title: Coalesce popup rerenderLog via requestAnimationFrame
Refactor addTool to use a mcp.ToolOption builder for intent
description:
main.go:99-118 addTool() reaches into tool.InputSchema.Properties and tool.InputSchema.Required to splice in the intent param *after* construction. Works because mcp-go currently exposes those as exported maps/slices, but it bypasses the library's WithString(...) builder option that every other parameter uses.

Costs:
1. If mcp-go ever changes InputSchema to an opaque type or a validated builder, every addTool call site breaks at once.
2. Schema-augmentation logic lives in main.go rather than next to the tool definition — a reader of tools_browser.go has to know there's invisible state added elsewhere.

Fix: ship a tiny helper that returns a mcp.ToolOption:

  func intentParam() mcp.ToolOption {
    return mcp.WithString("intent", mcp.Required(), mcp.Description(intentParamDescription))
  }

Then call sites become:
  s.AddTool(mcp.NewTool("click", intentParam(), mcp.WithString("selector", ...), ...), handler)

Drops the addTool wrapper entirely. Idiomatic. Same enforcement.

Start AFTER PR #3 lands (it has — fa43969). No coordination needed; this is purely Go.

Found by: architecture-strategist review of PR #3.

title: Landing index.html <title> still uses the old tagline
description: The <title> tag still says 'TurboWeb MCP — your browser, your agent, at the speed of intent.' That was the pre-A pick; we agreed on 'Real browsers. Real cursors. Real agents.' for the on-page H1 but never updated the document title. Update <title> + the og:title meta to match.

title: Consider leading-edge debounce variant for broadcastClientsToBrowsers
description:
ws.go now uses trailing-edge debounce (100ms) for broadcastClientsToBrowsers. Side-effect: a newly-connected agent waits up to 100ms before its label appears in the popup. Imperceptible in practice — popup loads in ~50ms — but if first-byte feel ever becomes a complaint, a leading-edge variant fires immediately and suppresses for 100ms afterwards.

Implementation: track lastFire time.Time; fire immediately if delta > debounce window, else schedule trailing.

Found by: performance-oracle review of PR #4.
